content: Do you know how our bodies know what to do? It's like they have a special set of instructions that tell them how to grow, how to move, and how to stay healthy. These instructions are carried by tiny messengers called mRNA, or messenger RNA. mRNA is like a special delivery truck that brings the instructions from one part of the cell to another. It's made up of a long chain of letters that spell out the instructions, kind of like a secret code. When the mRNA reaches its destination, it tells the cell what to do, like "make more muscle" or "fight off a germ." The cell then reads the instructions and follows them, which helps our bodies work properly. It's a pretty cool system, and it's happening inside our bodies all the time!
